新能源装机占比突破50%
能源边缘控制如何从"连接"走向"智能"
余海龙讲到，截至2025年底中国新能源装机历史性成为电力主体，非化石能源发电量达50%，"沙戈荒"基地与分布式能源并举，能源系统从"源随荷动"向"源网荷储"深度互动的去中心化智能体系演进。面对新能源间歇性与波动性挑战，他强调AI+边缘计算是破局核心，通过数据采集预处理、实时分析控制、预测性维护三种模式，将"被动维修"转为"主动预防"。目前行业成效显著：电网无人机巡检可识别46种缺陷、准确率超80%，石油石化2秒内识别抽油机故障、准确率95%，煤炭行业实现无人驾驶矿卡常态化。
在国产化方面，余海龙分享到万创科技自2014年布局飞腾、瑞芯微等方案，自主研发的VantronOS物联网操作系统已实现在消防安防、AGV控制器、电力通讯等领域的国产替代。目前，万创科技已推出从0.5Tops到150Tops的边缘算力产品矩阵，并在能源领域落地了EMS储能一体机、可拓展算力的风电边缘AI控制器等方案，以"硬件创造产品，软件创造价值"的理念助力能源行业智能化升级。
AI+能源边缘底座：国产化平台如何支撑现场智能
张康 硬件部及系统软件部总监
成都万创科技股份有限公司
国产化边缘底座的六大核心能力
张康阐述了万创AI+能源边缘底座六大核心能力。全域感知通过电气量、非电气量传感器及特种巡检设备，构建从宏观电网到微观设备的实时采集网络，为数字孪生与预测性维护提供基础；异构融合兼容全品类硬件，内置协议引擎实现IEC61850、Modbus等无缝转换，打破"设备烟囱"，达成"一网统管"。万创基于TSN协议构建能源工业通信底座，通过gPTP纳秒级同步、时隙调度与帧抢占实现有界超低时延，FRER零丢包与200微秒无缝切换为AI视觉、机器人巡检筑牢底座，实时控制覆盖亚毫秒到秒级多层次时延需求。
同时，万创提出"轻量化小模型+本地大模型"分层算力架构，小模型承担毫秒级实时监测，大模型作为"智慧大脑"负责态势研判。在供应链方面，万创已构建从核心主控到存储、网络、电源芯片的完善国产体系，通过电源与信号完整性仿真、整机热仿真及强电磁防护设计等设计保障，配合振动、老化、EMC等测试验证，确保设备全生命周期稳定运行。
从芯片到生态：国产算力驱动能源智能化演进
佟羽 高级行业解决方案架构师
佟羽分享了飞腾作为国产CPU设计国家队在能源信创领域的实践成果。飞腾推出腾珑E系列、腾锐D系列、腾云S系列全谱系芯片，其中腾珑E2000采用大小核柔性架构，精准匹配电力、工控、PLC等嵌入式场景。在能源行业，飞腾芯片已在火电DCS、水电SCADA、风电PLC、燃气发电TCS、电网直流控保、油气管网PLC/RTU等领域实现规模化落地，覆盖华能、华电、国家管网集团等大型央企，为能源关键基础设施的自主可控提供了坚实的国产算力支撑。
从系统能力到场景适配：AI+能源的现场化路径
成都万创科技股份有限公司
破解AI落地"最后一公里"
从系统能力到场景适配的现场化路径
张津直指行业痛点：AI模型在云端训练完成，却难以高效部署到发电厂、变电站、风电场等现场边缘——硬件不匹配、算力不足、部署周期长、远程运维难，构成“最后一公里”困境。以云南电网输电线路巡检为例，高温高寒、强电磁干扰、算法识别率不足、分散维护不便等极端挑战，要求AI必须下沉到边缘侧才能释放价值。
万创科技推出 “AI边缘计算机+工业网关+BlueSphere”三位一体方案：AI边缘计算机提供0.5~150 TOPS多档算力，推理延迟≤50ms；工业网关支持10+种协议，完成异构数据采集与归一化；BlueSphere云边协同平台支持模型远程部署、OTA升级与版本回滚，无需人工干预。方案兼容ARM/X86，适配主流技术栈，数日即可完成部署。
针对能源行业高温高寒、强电磁干扰、通信条件差异大等挑战，万创具备核心技术优势：深度适配7种以上电力协议，控制响应抖动±50μs，单网关管理200终端，宽温-40℃~+85℃，MTBF超10万小时；自研VantronOS集成实时内核，超18个月零重启，已实现国际品牌替代。同时，万创拥有179个标准品，覆盖工业通讯、智慧显示、边缘智能和云平台，以定制化为主，通过模块组合快速响应不同项目需求。
从智能控制到智慧运营：能源项目数字化升级实践
同时，现场邀请到综合性头部能源企业大物流系统相关负责人杨工，他分享了能源项目从智能控制迈向智慧运营的数字化升级实践。以智慧物资安防、普货运输、过驳油作业三大场景为例，详细展示了系统如何通过"规则数字化—数据采集—AI智能体分析—风险分类—智能推送—事件处理"的闭环流程，以智能体校验替代人工监督，将管理从"被动响应"转变为"主动预警"。在这一体系中，万创BlueSphere网关管理平台、VantronOS操作系统与边缘计算网关发挥了关键的设备管理与数据采集作用，支撑了数智化体系的稳定运行，推动油气运营从被动响应走向主动预警。
除了集中的主题演讲，万创科技在茶歇与产品演示区营造了轻松的交流氛围，现场重点展示了面向煤炭、新能源等能源现场的核心产品，包括边缘计算网关、算力主板、AIBOX、智能触显终端等。与会技术专家围绕TSN确定性通信、边缘AI模型部署、多协议接入等实际场景展开深入交流，万创团队结合“能力驱动场景落地”的理念，针对客户在国产化替代、边缘算力选型、项目交付效率等方面的需求，提供了覆盖设备互联、实时本地决策、可视化协同的全链路技术建议。尤其在无人巡检、设备预测性维护、AI智能监测及场站智能控制等能源应用领域，团队给出了定制化的可落地方案，现场讨论气氛热烈。
本次活动充分展现了产业链上下游协同创新的力量。从万创的边缘算力平台与工业通信底座，到飞腾的全国产芯片支撑，再到中海油的油气运营数字化实践，共同勾勒出AI+能源从云端走向现场的清晰路径。在国家"十五五"AI+能源战略与信创全面替代的双重驱动下，万创科技将继续携手生态伙伴，以更可靠的工程级设计、更协同的软硬件一体化能力、更高效的模块化交付体系，助力能源客户在智能化变革中行稳致远。
成都万创科技股份有限公司成立于 2002年，深耕嵌入式技术领域二十余年，拥有国际一流的研发团队与完善的自主研发体系。
公司产品涵盖边缘AI智能硬件、工业网络通信设备、工业显示终端及工业互联网云平台，广泛服务于工业自动化、机器人、能源电力与智能装备等关键行业。
作为国内领先的嵌入式与 AIoT方案提供商，万创科技长期为全球 500 强及行业头部企业提供高性能、可定制的嵌入式与物联网产品化解决方案。

title: 土地、限电、电价三重压力下，光伏投资热退潮！
content:
限电、交易电价、土地成本的不确定性，让
这项长达20年以上的固定资产投资项目，变成一个“低收益、高风险”的长周期投资项目，企业投资热情快速下降。2026年前5个月国内光伏新增装机同比下降70%。
整体市场体量下降的情况下，生存成为光伏设备企业的第一要务；企业很难脱离“白热化”市场竞争的现实，将反内卷工作真正落到实处。
一、光伏反内卷一年半，为何组件中标价再次跌破0.7元/W！
近日，浙能2.52GW组件集采公布中标结果，最低价0.685元/W，再次跌破0.7元/W！毫无疑问，这一价格低于光伏组件的全成本价！
更值得注意的是，报出这一价格的并非三线小厂，而是稳居一线的天合光能！
也不仅仅是天合光能，所有中标企业的报价，都低于中国光伏行业协会2026年年初公布的“组件全成本+税+质保费”的价格（当前银价约13~14元/kg）。《CPIA：银价20元/g时，组件平均全成本0.824元/W》
从2024年12月的中央经济工作会议上提出综合整治“内卷式”竞争开始，光伏行业已经开展了超过一年半的反内卷工作。
为何产品价格还如此“卷”？为何企业间竞争还如此“卷”？
“卷”，是下行市场下的无奈之举！
二、国内光伏市场寒气逼人！
根据国家能源局的数据，2026年1~5月，光伏新增装机
59.59GW，同比大幅下降70%！
新增装机下降只是结果，背后原因是企业光伏项目投资热情的大幅下降！
1）光伏项目开发意愿下降
根据各省公开下发的新能源项目指标分配结果，2026年1~6月，光伏项目的规模明显低于风电项目。而在河北省发改委刚公布的《2026年河北省部分风电光伏项目竞配清单》中，只有1.93GW的风电项目，甚至没有一个光伏项目。
2）获得指标后，最终放弃开发
不仅仅是新开发的项目减少，2026年上半年，许多已经获得项目指标的企业也放弃建设。
贵州、福建、山西、陕西、新疆、宁夏等各级政府，清退了超过4GW、超期为开工的光伏项目。
甚至有的项目已经开展的EPC招标阶段，但由于政策、土地等原因，终止EPC招标。初步统计，发终止公告的项目规模就已经超过2.2GW，如下表所示。
表：2026年上半年半部分EPC终止的项目
企业都不愿意开发、建设光伏项目了！这也意味着，未来光伏的新增装机大概率会持续下降。
三、三重不确定性，让光伏项目低收益、高风险！
与风电项目不同，光伏项目占地面积大，土地相关成本问题尤为突出。然而，各省对土地税收的执行情况一直存在差异。即使同一地区，政策也会有变化。在之前的文章中曾经介绍过。《》
2017年12月，内蒙古为促进光伏行业发展，出台内财税〔2017〕2010号文（原文件附后），明确光伏用地的税收优惠政策。
2025年9月，内蒙古财政厅、内蒙古税务局发布文件明确：自2025年12月31日，废止内财税〔2017〕2010号文件！即内蒙古的土地税收优惠将被取消！
2026年6月底，山西某光伏领跑者基地项目的投资企业，收到地方税务的通知。通知中提到：通过对企业有关涉税事项进行风险核查后，发现:未足额申报耕地占用税、未足额申报城镇土地使用税。通知企业进行自查补正，自行补正申报，补缴税款、调整账目。
然而，2017年领跑者基地招标时，当地税务部门曾经出示红头的“承诺函”，承诺：光伏领跑基地所选场址，不在县城，建制镇和工矿区范围内，不属于征收城镇土地使用税范围;占用少量的耕地，按基地项目规划，采用农业种植大棚加装光伏组件发电模式，不改变农业用地性质，不属于征收耕地占用税范围。
2017年项目招标时红头文件承诺不征收，2026年又要求企业补缴。政策的变化，让土地成本存在巨大的不确定性。
光伏项目占地动辄上百公顷，土地相关税费政策的变动，将会带来项目成本的巨大变化，使项目收益率明显低于投资决策时的预测值。
2026年3月12日，京能集团下属的乡城联合光伏开发有限公司给四川省发改委提交《关于乡城联合光伏开发有限公司正勉
因发电能力受限导致经营困难的情况说明》，提到：
3月16日，能源局电力调度处回复处理意见：已线下沟通，达成一致意见。
光伏扶贫电站作为一项民生工程，一向享受各种优惠政策。扶贫电站的限电问题可以当做特殊电站得到解决，但其他普通的
则很难享受这种特殊照顾。在之前的文章中，也根据9家央企下属上市公司的财报，统计了各家风电、光伏项目的发电小时数变。2025年，各家的发电小时数都有不同程度的下降。多家财报中提到限电损失明显增加。
表：9家上市公司风电、光伏年利用小时数及变化（单位：小时）
在一文中介绍了7个省风电、光伏现货交易的价格。可以看出：由于光伏项目出力时间集中，各省光伏项目的现货交易电价普遍低于风电项目，低于煤基准电价。而且，从山东、山西、广东等第一批开展现货交易的省份来看，随着光伏渗透率提高，光伏现货平均电价逐年走低。
虽然目前有机制电价托底，但机制电量并不能覆盖100%的发电量。以甘肃为例，普通存量光伏项目，机制电量不超过37.5小时，大概只能覆盖2~3%的发电量。
如果只有少部分电量的电价相对确定，大部分电量电价未知、且有下降趋势，但下降幅度很难预测；那项目的上网电价就有很大的不确定性。
4、三重不确定性叠加，带来投资高风险
土地相关税费政策变化，让光伏项目成本（支出）充满不确定性；
项目的限电形势日益严峻、交易电价低，让光伏项目的收入充满不确定性。
然而，光伏项目投资周期是20~30年。在20~30年的周期里，三重不确定因素叠加，就让光伏项目成为一项高风险投资。
高风险需要高收益来对冲。那光伏项目收益有多高呢？
2026年4月10日，甘肃能源发布审议通过《关于投资建设甘肃庆阳东数西算产业园区绿电聚合试点项目二期工程100万千瓦新能源项目的议案》，项目的资本金收益率仅为2.17%！
而大多数央国企光伏项目投决的收益率要求也在6%左右。
资本金收益率6%左右，一旦项目的土地成本、发电量、上网电价等条件发生变动，项目很可能面临亏损。
2025年，是中国光伏项目投资的高光时刻！全年新增装机达到316.6GW！这可能将是最近几年的极值。
2026年以来，光伏行业的投资热度肉眼可见下降；1~5月份的新增装机，甚至低于2024年。然而，更让人担忧的是，从目前项目申报、开工情况来看，这种下降趋势将会持续！
在这种市场收缩的情况下，供过于求会更加明显，企业竞争必然白热化！企业的销售价格很难抬高，“反内卷”工作也会更加任重道远！

title: 大唐陕西100MW光伏项目环评公示
content:
7月3日，陕西省安康市生态环境局发布大唐平利县10万千瓦光伏项目环评受理公示。
大唐平利县10万千瓦光伏项目位于安康市平利县广佛镇、洛河镇，永久占地面积为7078㎡，总投资42000万元，施工工期为12个月。该项目为“茶光互补”太阳能光伏发电项目，交流侧容量为100MW，直流侧安装容量为112.46MWp，共安装17.3万块650Wp的N型单晶双面双玻组件。
该项目建设单位为大唐富平热电有限公司，其控股股东为大唐陕西发电有限公司。后者为大唐集团控股子公司。

title: 越南光伏装了30GW，为什么还在限电？
content:
越南光伏产业正在经历一个诡异的时刻：工厂机器日夜不停，硅片、电池、组件从生产线上一批批下来，装箱发往全球——但翻开行业账本，超过一半的产能建在"政策赌局"上。
光伏是越南近十年最成功的招商引资故事。从2019年开始，天合光能、晶科能源、隆基绿能、晶澳科技——几乎所有中国光伏巨头都在越南设了厂。到2025年底，越南光伏组件年产能突破
，相当于全球需求的约10%。
但故事的另一面是：美国对越南光伏产品加征的反倾销/反补贴关税叠加后
，越南国内电网消纳能力极其有限，而全球光伏组件价格在2024-2025年暴跌了约40%。
产能建起来了，买家却在一个接一个地消失。
据越南计划投资部（MPI）和各地工业区管委会的公开数据，越南光伏制造业分布呈现"北重南轻"格局——超过80%的产能集中在
三个北部省份的工业园区内。仅北江省的光伏产业集群，就聚集了天合光能、晶科能源、隆基绿能三家头部企业，合计组件产能超过15GW。
更值得关注的是产能的集中度。据BloombergNEF和PV InfoLink的行业统计，前五大企业在越南的产能占比超过
。这意味着——这些工厂的产能规划，绝大多数是服务于同一个出口目的地：
而美国市场的政策风向，从2022年起就一直在收紧。
2022年3月，美国商务部启动对柬埔寨、马来西亚、泰国、越南四国光伏产品的
——核心指控是：中国光伏企业在东南亚设厂，只是把硅片从中国运过去组装，本质上是"洗产地"以避开对中国光伏的双反关税。
2023年8月，美国商务部做出
：认定天合光能、晶科、隆基等八家中国光伏企业通过在越南等国的工厂规避关税，对其产品征收反倾销/反补贴税。叠加后税率在
2024年，拜登政府虽然给了一个两年豁免窗口期（至2024年6月），但窗口期结束后，关税全面恢复。到2025-2026年，美国光伏进口商面临的实际到岸成本已是东南亚组件出厂价的
伍德麦肯兹（Wood Mackenzie）的一份行业报告直接点明：
越南光伏产能对美国的出口路径，已经从"高速路"变成了"独木桥"。
▎案例一：天合光能（北江）——产能最大，转身最难
天合光能在北江省的工厂是越南最大的光伏组件生产基地之一，年产能约6-8GW。美国关税落地后，天合的应对策略是
——在印尼、印度新建工厂，把越南工厂的部分产能转向
市场。但转型成本高昂：新市场开拓缓慢，越南工厂开工率一度降至
。据PV Tech报道，天合在2025年第四季度出现了越南工厂的资产减值计提。
▎案例二：晶科能源——"硅片本地化"赌对了
晶科能源在北宁省的工厂早在2021年就布局了
——不只是做组装，而是把硅片这一核心环节放在了越南。这让晶科在2023年美国反规避调查中获得了更大的合规空间。据公司2025年年报披露，越南工厂的组件出货量仍保持在5GW以上，且
20%转向了欧洲和亚太市场
。策略核心：不把鸡蛋放在美国一个篮子里。
▎案例三：First Solar——不一样的玩法
美国公司First Solar在越南胡志明市附近的工厂使用的是
技术——与中国企业主流的晶硅技术不同，因此
。2024年First Solar宣布追加投资扩大越南产能至3.5GW。这提醒了一个关键事实：
在越南做光伏，技术的差异化本身就是关税护城河。
很多人说：美国不买，越南自己装啊。越南阳光充足，光伏大有可为。
但现实是：越南第八次电力发展规划（PDP8）虽然大幅提高了可再生能源目标——到2030年光伏装机目标是
（其中集中式约8.7GW、屋顶约9.9GW），但截至2025年底，越南已安装的光伏总容量约为
简单算一笔账：越南光伏制造产能
。即使国内需求翻倍到6GW，产能利用率也才20%。产能与市场之间存在一个
越南电网的消纳能力严重滞后
。2019-2020年FIT抢装潮已经给电网带来过教训——中南部大量
"晒太阳"发不出电，因为输电线路跟不上。虽然2024-2025年越南电力公司（EVN）在加速输电线路建设，但电网扩容的速度远跟不上装机速度。
出路一：从"出口美国"到"出口全球"
欧洲、中东、拉美、非洲——全球光伏需求在2025年已超过400GW，美国只是其中约40GW的一块。欧洲的年需求约60-70GW，且在推动"去中国化"的供应链多元化。越南工厂如果能通过碳足迹认证和供应链溯源，在欧洲比中国本土工厂有优势。关键门槛：
欧盟碳边境调节机制（CBAM）
要求提供完整的碳足迹数据。
出路二：参与越南国内能源转型
越南PDP8规划了大量新能源装机，尤其是
。北江、北宁的工业园区本身就聚集了三星、富士康等用电大户——工厂屋顶装光伏是最直接的消纳场景。政策方向：
DPPA（直接购电协议）
机制开放后，企业可以直接从
购电，绕过EVN的统一电网，这给大型工商业光伏创造了新的商业模式。
出路三：向产业链上下游延伸
目前越南光伏制造集中在"电池片+组件"中游环节。上游的硅料、硅棒几乎空白，下游的光伏支架、逆变器、储能配套也有大量空白。这些领域的关税风险远低于组件出口——例如光伏支架和储能系统目前不在美国光伏双反的征税范围内。
坑一：产能不等于竞争力。
30GW产能中，相当比例是老一代PERC技术产线。全球正在快速转向TOPCon和HJT技术。PERC产线的资产贬值速度可能快于你的折旧速度——某越南光伏工厂的PERC产线在两年前估值10亿人民币，现在残值不到4亿。
坑二：美国的政策不是"紧一阵松一阵"——是单行道。
不管谁当总统，美国光伏制造业回流是两党共识。《通胀削减法案》（IRA）给本土制造的补贴力度极大——美国本土产的组件每瓦补贴7美分，电池每瓦补贴4美分。东南亚工厂在成本上已经没有绝对优势。
坑三：北江/北宁的用工成本在快速上升。
光伏工厂高度集中在三个省，用工竞争激烈。2023年产业工人月薪约600-800万越盾（约1500-2000元），到2025年底已涨到1000-1300万越盾（约2500-3200元）。再加上越南社保缴费比例从2025年起上调——劳动力成本优势正在被侵蚀。

title: 部分分布式光伏项目并网时间存在异议
content:
日前，昆明市发展和改革委员会发布通知，对部分分布式新能源项目全容量并网时间认定异议项目进行更正并再次公示。
据悉，根据《云南省能源局关于优化新能源项目全容量并网事宜的通知》（云能源新能〔2026〕148号）要求，昆明市发展和改革委员会已于2026年6月18日至28日对《昆明市2025年6月1日至2026年5月31日分布式新能源项目全容量并网时间清单》进行了公示。
公示期间，部分项目业主对清单所列内容提出异议。经昆明供电局核查，异议反馈内容属实。为确保认定结果公开、公平、公正，现将存在异议的项目信息予以更正并再次公示。

title: 阿特斯：原COO出任CEO！
content:
阿特斯阳光电力近期完成旗下全球电站开发主体Recurrent Energy管理层更迭，Dylan Marx接替离任的Ismael Guerrero出任这家子公司首席执行官，相关人事调整即刻落地。为保障业务平稳过渡，Guerrero会以非执行顾问身份持续配合公司各项工作，服务期限至2026年12月31日。
此番人事调整同步带来岗位变动，原本任职阿特斯阳光电力首席运营官的Marx选择卸任原有职务，全身心统筹Recurrent Energy整体发展。
长期参与Recurrent Energy全球业务运营管理的Marx，对这家企业的日常经营状况十分熟悉，过往也在运营管控工作中承担核心职责，阿特斯阳光电力方面也表示，依托他积累的行业实操经验与管理能力，能够充分带动子公司迈入全新增长阶段。
阿特斯阳光电力首席执行官Colin Parkin针对此次人事任命对外表态，集团始终会持续赋能Recurrent Energy的业务拓展，力求为合作方与各方利益相关方创造长期稳定收益。他还提到，Marx深耕内部业务、兼具全球化视野与成熟运营管理能力，完全有能力带领子公司稳步前行，同时也向Ismael Guerrero多年来为企业发展付出的努力表达了谢意。

title: Breaking Down the Limits of Solid-State Battery Performance
content:
Discover where the performance cliff lies in solid-state batteries and how chemistry choices impact energy density and scalability.
July 8, 2026
The question is no longer whether solid-state batteries hold potential, but where their performance limits lie.
mesh cube/iStock / Getty Images Plus
Solid-state batteries have long been heralded as the future of energy storage, promising breakthroughs in energy density, safety, and performance. As the field has matured, the initial wave of excitement has given way to a more nuanced understanding of the challenges and trade-offs involved. Researchers and manufacturers now grapple with the complex interplay of energy density, interface stability, stack pressure, and cost. The question is no longer whether solid-state batteries hold potential, but where their performance limits lie — and what happens when those limits are crossed.
Coverage of solid-state batteries has matured past the hype. The promise-versus-physics picture — energy density set against interface stability, stack pressure, and cost — is now well drawn. What it leaves open is a structural question: along the ladder of solid-state chemistries, where does cell performance actually break down? Sulfide all-solid cells already report strong numbers. The aggressive chemistries chased for higher energy density are another matter. Somewhere between them there is a cliff the field gestures at but rarely locates. Locating it changes how the bets should be sequenced.
Learn How an iPhone Battery Is Manufactured
Solid-state performance does not degrade gently as chemistries advance. It holds, then drops. The reasons are threshold effects, not gradients: swapping sulfide electrolytes for halide composites changes interface chemistry wholesale, and putting lithium metal on a lithium-rich manganese-oxide cathode introduces plating and interface instabilities a sulfide all-solid stack never sees. Each is a wall, not a ramp. Below it, metrics look healthy; cross it, and they step-change. That is the signature of a cliff — and why progress can look smooth right up to the point it isn't.
To find that wall, I applied a cross-industry yield-cliff diagnostic — a method validated across thirteen manufacturing industries that places the breakpoint on a normalized architectural axis — to a census of 21 solid-state cells drawn only from vendor disclosures and peer-reviewed sources. The method and the census are open-access, so the figures are checkable.
The result is specific: on an axis running from sulfide all-solid cells through halide composites to lithium-metal-on-LRMO chemistries, the fitted breakpoint lands at 0.78, with a bootstrap 95% confidence interval of 0.71 to 0.84. Sulfide all-solid cells sit below it; the halide-composite and lithium-metal chemistries sit above it. The method matters for a second reason: applied to transitions that lack a cliff — memory-chip stack heights, quantum gate fidelity — it returns a smooth slope instead. Because it can tell a step-change from a gradient, its solid-state verdict carries weight.
Video Explores Why US Battery Manufacturing Lags Behind China—And What It Will Take to Catch Up
Based on the solid-state census (n = 21, Tier A). The fitted cliff sits at f = 0.778 (bootstrap 95% CI 0.71-0.84), the boundary between sulfide all-solid cells (f about 0.65-0.75) and halide-composite / lithium-metal-on-LRMO chemistries (f about 0.85-0.95). Curve illustrative; cliff position and chemistry-family ranges are from the census. Courtesy of Zenodo, CC BY 4.0.
Here is the uncomfortable part. The chemistries the industry is pushing toward for higher energy density — halide electrolytes, lithium metal on high-capacity cathodes — are precisely the ones on the far side of the cliff. The energy-density ambition and the performance step-change are the same boundary. Meanwhile the cells posting healthy pilot numbers today sit below it: Gotion's sulfide all-solid pilot line reports about 90%, and Factorial's semi-solid platform climbed from 10% to 85% — both pre-cliff chemistries. Strong numbers there say little about what happens once a program crosses into the aggressive ones.
The axis is not a single path, so the cliff does not fall in one place for every program. A manufacturer committed to sulfide all-solid is operating below the breakpoint and can scale on largely familiar terms. A program reaching for lithium-metal-on-LRMO energy density is committing to the far side, where interface and plating behavior step-change. Two developers that have both gone solid-state can therefore be exposed very differently, depending on how aggressive a chemistry they are actually chasing. The label is not the cliff; the position on the chemistry axis is.
7 Game-Changing Battery Technologies
If the cliff is a chemistry boundary, the order of operations changes. Moving from sulfide to halide or lithium metal is not only an energy-density choice — it is the point at which performance risk step-changes, and it should be qualified as such before a line is committed. Know which side of the boundary a chemistry sits on, and sequence the aggressive bets behind the evidence rather than ahead of it. A located cliff is what tells you how far a chemistry roadmap actually reaches — and which step is the one that bites.
Sang Bong Song
Principal Analyst, Song Industry Research
Sang Bong Song is the Principal Analyst at Song Industry Research in Seoul, where he develops cross-industry diagnostics for manufacturing yield. His work applies a single framework across thirteen industries — from semiconductors and batteries to pharmaceuticals — to locate where production yield breaks.
See more from
Sang Bong Song

title: Flower Infrastructure buys 112 MW German BESS portfolio from Chint Group - Energy Storage
content:
Chint Solar Europe has agreed to sell and deliver seven BESS projects to Flower Infrastructure Technologies across the German federal states of Thuringia, Lower Saxony, Saxony-Anhalt, Saxony, and Hesse, with individual project capacities ranging from 6 MW to 48 MW and storage durations of three to four hours.
All projects have secured grid connections and required permits, Chint Solar Europe said. Commissioning is scheduled throughout 2027.
Under the terms of the agreement, Flower Infrastructure Technologies will own, finance, operate, and commercialize the assets. Chint Solar Europe will deliver the projects under an engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) agreement, including long-term operations and maintenance services.
The deal adds to Chint’s broader battery storage pipeline in Europe, which the company said includes 168 MW/513 MWh under construction contracts and more than 1 GW in development. Chint said the deal reflects its shift from a solar-only developer to an integrated energy infrastructure provider.
“Germany’s rapidly growing flexibility market requires storage assets that can be deployed quickly,” said Bernd Kipping, Chint Solar Europe’s vice president of innovation, finance, and procurement. “By combining mature project development with EPC delivery, we reduce execution risks for investors and accelerate the deployment of battery storage infrastructure.”
Some of the projects will be Flower’s first operational assets in Germany, with commissioning set to begin as early as the first quarter of 2027, said Flower Infrastructure Technologies CEO John Diklev.
Osborne Clarke advised Chint Solar Europe on the transaction.

title: Grenergy launches auction for 960 GWh of battery storage supply in Chile - Energy Storage
content:
Spanish renewable energy company Grenergy has launched a reverse auction to contract 1.5 TWh of annual renewable electricity supply in Chile through its subsidiary GR Power. The offering includes 960 GWh per year of battery-stored electricity for nighttime delivery and 540 GWh per year of solar generation for daytime supply, with deliveries scheduled to begin between the second and third quarters of 2028.
The auction targets generators, electricity retailers and large energy consumers seeking long-term power supply agreements backed by Grenergy’s solar and battery storage assets.
Participants may bid for blocks of 20 GWh and choose contract terms of six, eight, 10, 12 or 15 years. The auction will remain open until July 22 following a consultation period that ended on July 7, with registration managed through the Match Energía trading platform.
Grenergy said this is the third renewable energy auction it has organized directly. The company said it became the first clean energy producer in Latin America to use the reverse auction model in 2022 to expand access to long-term renewable power contracts.
Electricity will be supplied from Grenergy’s solar plants and battery energy storage systems in northern and central Chile. The company said its Oasis Atacama platform in the Atacama Desert already includes 2.5 GW of solar capacity and 14 GWh of battery storage, while the Oasis Central project under development in central Chile will add 1.4 GW of solar generation and 5.1 GWh of battery storage.
Grenergy said it intends to replicate the hybrid solar-plus-storage model in other international markets, particularly in Europe.
The company said its global development portfolio includes 71 GWh of battery storage and 12 GW of solar projects.

title: Grid expansion, storage, flexibility lag behind Europe’s renewables growth - Energy Storage
content:
rids, storage, flexibility and demand-side electrification are not keeping pace with Europe’s growing renewables fleet, according to a new report by the European Climate Neutrality Observatory (ECNO).
The think tank’s
2026 flagship report
assesses Europe’s progress toward climate neutrality across key sectors. It found that variable renewables, consisting of solar and wind energy, reached 30% of EU electricity generation in 2025 but says their share rose “far too slowly” between 2020 and 2025 to remain on track towards a targeted 58% share by 2030, a benchmark identified in the European Commission’s impact assessment as the level consistent with its proposed 2040 climate target.
Curtailment and flexibility limitations are highlighted as key reasons as to why the share of variable renewable electricity is rising slower than installed capacity figures would suggest.
“Our analysis points to the need for faster and better coordinated grid expansion, including shorter grid connection times, so that renewable electricity and electrification projects are not delayed by infrastructure bottlenecks,” Eike Velten, director of the climate programm at Ecologic Institute and lead author of the report, told
pv magazine.
“This should cover distribution grids explicitly, not only transmission grids.”
Findings in the report add that while grid investments
have increased
since 2020, they are not growing fast enough. It says that in the distribution grid alone, annual investment growth of between 12% to 25% is needed.
ECNO’s report explains that the framework for grids remains fragmented across the EU, national regulatory and member state competences, with financing and planning decisions largely in the hands of member states.
It adds that EU recent guidance and legislative proposals covering faster procedures for grids and storage, alongside tariff design that incentivises end-use flexibility, are “important but not yet enough”.
“The planned 2026 White Paper on electricity market integration will be important for advancing EU market integration, improving the integration of renewables and flexibility, and reducing system costs,” the report continues. “At the same time…member states need to urgently align grid planning with renewable deployment, accelerate permitting and invest in transmission and distribution.”
Smart meter roll-out across Europe is also lagging, the report adds, blocking end users from demand-side flexibility. The report urges member states to complete smart meter deployment alongside introducing dynamic tariffs and removing barriers for small actors and aggregators. “Only the combination unlocks meaningful demand-side flexibility,” it says.
EU member states need to ensure timely and effective implementation of smart meters so consumers and businesses can shift electricity use to times when renewable power is available and electricity prices are low.
“This should be linked to dynamic tariffs which should be made available by now by member states but cannot be used if they have no smart meters installed,” he added.
While battery storage capacity across Europe is increasing, the report says the growth remains below what the European Commission expects to be required by 2030. According to capacity-market data from 2020-2024, fossil capacity, and in particular gas, increased much faster than demand response and battery storage.
“A more positive development is visible since 2023, with renewable and battery capacities growing more strongly than fossil capacities,” the report continues. “Still, many capacity mechanisms still favour gas plants which are better placed to meet adequacy requirements, while batteries are not yet rewarded at the scale needed to shift flexibility provision decisively away from fossil fuels.”
Velten said that strengthened support schemes for non-fossil flexibility are needed, including battery storage, to better integrate renewables into the power system. “Capacity mechanisms should allow and promote storage and demand response,” he told
Earlier this year, a report from Ember found major grid bottlenecks in transmission and distribution networks across Europe, with a
shortfall of around 120 GW
between planned renewables expansion and available grid capacity across the majority of EU member states.

title: Peak Energy to build 4 GWh grid-scale sodium-ion BESS factory in California - Energy Storage
content:
Peak Energy’s 183,000 square foot facility in Sacramento, California, will be capable of producing up to 4 GWh of sodium-ion batteries annually.
It will cost around $71 million. The company, which is partially headquartered in the San Francisco Bay Area, said the investment will create hundreds of jobs and stimulate the local economy.
Peak Energy has secured more than 6 GWh of customer commitments to 2030, and it expects to enter production and begin shipments in the first quarter of 2027. The fast-growing company only completed its Series A funding round in 2024.
“America needs energy storage that is lower cost, more affordable, more reliable and purpose-built to meet the demand coming onto the grid,” said Landon Mossburg, CEO of Peak Energy. “This facility is proof that America can lead not only in inventing the technology, but in building it at scale. With our manufacturing facility in Sacramento, we’re enabling American energy innovation to lower electric bills while creating high quality jobs.”
The team has spent the past few months making high-profile deals with other companies to scale its sodium-ion energy storage technology. The factory will help realize these commitments and increase deployment of its sodium-ion systems for high-demand sectors in the United States, like data centers and industrial manufacturing.
The startup has already announced the
successful deployment and operation
of its first grid-scale sodium-ion system in Watkins, Colorado. At 3.5 MWh of storage capacity, the system is one of the country’s biggest sodium-ion batteries.
In March 2026, the company
revealed plans
to deploy a sodium-ion battery with RWE Americas in Eastern Wisconson.
Peak Energy also has an agreement with Jupiter Power to deliver approximately
4.75 GWh of storage
between 2027 and 2030, and a
1.5 GWh deal
with Energy Vault to incorporate its sodium-ion technology into an energy storage platform for AI data centers.
In June, Peak Energy signed a partnership with GM Ventures, the investment arm of General Motors to combine GM’s sodium-ion cell technology with its proprietary energy storage platform.
in July 2025, Peak Energy CCO and President Cameron Dales said the company’s system costs less to operate and is more durable and reliable compared to lithium-ion phosphate batteries.
The Sacramento site was chosen following a nationwide site selection process. Peak Energy was awarded a $10.5 million Californian tax credit in May to aid its local expansion in Sacramento and following the factory announcement, local government representatives hailed the investment.
“The future of energy is being built in California,” said Dee Dee Myers, Senior Advisor to Governor Newsom and Director of the Governor’s Office of Business and Economic Development (GO-Biz).
“Peak Energy’s decision to select Sacramento is exactly the kind of advanced manufacturing investment that reflects the strengths of our region,” said Barry Broome, President and CEO of the Greater Sacramento Economic Council. “Sacramento has become a destination for innovative companies looking to grow, manufacture and invest for the long term. This project will create opportunities for our local businesses, working families and communities while ensuring the benefits of California’s energy transformation are felt right here in Sacramento for generations to come.”

title: Polaris signs CFE agreement for 250 MW solar, 192 MWh battery portfolio in Mexico - Energy Storage
content:
Polaris Renewable Energy has signed a mixed investment agreement with Mexico’s state-owned utility CFE for three solar-plus-storage projects totaling 249 MW of solar capacity and 61.6 MW/192 MWh of battery energy storage.
The agreement covers projects in Quintana Roo, Tlaxcala and Sinaloa with an estimated investment of $217 million, excluding grid interconnection and network upgrade costs.
The agreement was signed on July 3 by Polaris’ Mexican project entities and Banca Mifel, acting as trustee on behalf of CFE. It establishes a 30-year framework for the joint development, financing, construction, ownership and operation of the projects.
The largest project is Los Girasoles in Quintana Roo, which will combine 132.57 MW of solar capacity with a 33 MW/101.5 MWh battery energy storage system. Polaris expects the plant to generate about 249,239 MWh annually, with an estimated investment of $120 million and commercial operation targeted by Nov. 28, 2028.
The Solar Energía Tres Hermanos project in Tlaxcala will include 91.06 MW of solar generation and a 22.5 MW/71.6 MWh battery system. Annual generation is projected at 198,485 MWh, with an estimated investment of $78 million and commercial operation expected by Dec. 1, 2028.
The Don Humberto project in Sinaloa will feature 25.3 MW of solar capacity paired with a 6.1 MW/18.9 MWh battery system. Polaris forecasts annual generation of 47,173 MWh, with a $19 million investment and commercial operation planned by April 1, 2028.
Following execution of the agreement, the parties will finalize remaining documentation, including power purchase agreements, trust agreements, management services agreements, operation and maintenance contracts, and other project documents. Polaris said energy and storage pricing will be updated once project budgets incorporate interconnection and grid reinforcement costs.
Polaris announced in June that the three projects had been selected to advance to the final negotiation stage under CFE’s Mixed Development Program. The first tender selected 37 renewable energy projects, including 33 solar projects totaling about 6.71 GW and four wind projects totaling about 700 MW.

title: Remote Australian community deploys 19 kW/61.4 kWh battery-backed power system - Energy Storage
content:
A 19 kW/61.4 kWh remote area power system (RAPS) has been installed almost 1,000 km northwest of Adelaide in South Australia’s remote Great Victoria Desert region, to meet most of the
community’s electricity needs
Tjirilya Aboriginal Corp. (TAC), which represents the Mt. Willoughby Station Traditional Owners, had relied on an aging off-grid power system that depended heavily on a diesel generator to meet daily electricity needs. The system consumed increasing amounts of fuel and raised ongoing reliability concerns.
The system, located 251 km south of the Northern Territory border, is built around a single-phase Victron MultiPlus-II inverter/charger that delivers 15 kVA of power while allowing for future expansion as energy needs grow.
MyEnergy Engineering
, an Adelaide-based system designer and installer, said it includes 40 roof-mounted
panels, installed using a Schletter mounting system and supported by 61.44 kWh of ZYC Energy battery storage to ensure reliable power availability.
The system was integrated into the existing infrastructure, including the existing generator, which now operates as backup only when required.
Key components include an SMA Sunny Boy 5 kW inverter, Victron SmartSolar MPPT RS 450/100-Tr, Victron SmartSolar MPPT RS 450/200-MC4 and Victron Ekrano GX.
The system was remotely designed, pre-built and fully tested at MyEnergy headquarters, housed within a large weather- and vermin-resistant custom steel cabinet. This approach allowed for efficient transport to site and reduced installation time in the remote location.
TAC is responsible for land management across the region for ecological, environmental and cultural heritage preservation. The new system provides a
dependable power
supply to support day-to-day living and conservation operations. Reliance on the generator has been significantly reduced, keeping it as a backup as needed.

title: Trinasolar targets up to 1.6 GWh of BESS deliveries in Italy in 2026 - Energy Storage
content:
Trinasolar plans to supply between 1.4 GWh and 1.6 GWh of BESS capacity in Italy this year, according to Gonzalo de la Viña, the company’s president for Europe, Latin America and the Caribbean.
He said the estimate reflects products scheduled for delivery rather than signed contracts.
De la Viña told
pv magazine Italia
that Italy is Trinasolar’s second-largest BESS market after the United Kingdom and its third-largest solar module market after Spain and Germany.
He said the company is awaiting the results of Italy’s MACSE storage capacity auction after the application window opened this week. He added that Trinasolar expects to supply about 2 GW of solar modules in Italy this year. The company estimates its market share at between 15% and 20% in both Italy’s BESS and solar module markets.
De la Viña said Trinasolar is now assessing the possibility of building a BESS manufacturing facility in Europe but has no plans to establish a solar module factory on the continent. He said manufacturing costs in Europe remain 30% to 50% higher than in China.
The executive said France and Spain are the leading candidates for a future BESS factory, following an assessment of political, economic and labor market conditions. He said Germany would be too expensive, while concerns over Hungary’s political environment have reduced its attractiveness.
De la Viña said Europe continues to lack a competitive battery supply chain and faces higher labor and operating costs, making it more economical for Trinasolar to import materials from China and Southeast Asia even after import duties.
He added that the company is diversifying its supply chain by sourcing power conversion systems from Spanish manufacturer Power Electronics while also offering its own China-made PCS products.
As part of its European strategy, Trinasolar also holds what it described as a small stake in the 5 GW HoloSolis solar manufacturing project in France, where it is providing technology licensing and technical support. The project aims to begin production in the third quarter of 2028, following completion of its current fundraising rounds.

title: BYD reveals prices for its new 1,500+ luxury EV sports car start at nearly $200K [Images]
content:
Peter Johnson
| Jul 9 2026 - 7:46 am PT
took the covers off its new drop-top electric sports car at the Goodwood FOS. The Denza Z packs over 1,500 hp and can recharge in as little as 5 minutes, but it’s not cheap.
The Denza Z “marks a watershed moment for the global auto industry,” BYD said after introducing the electric supercar at the Beijing Auto Show in April.
BYD’s sub-brand, Denza, is preparing to go head-to-head with Europe’s top luxury automakers like Porsche, BMW, and Mercedes-Benz.
The Denza Z arrives as “the world’s first intelligent supercar,” according to BYD, delivering “shattering high performance,” 5-minute Flash Charging, and its latest breakthrough technology.
BYD announced that the electric supercar will be available in three variations: Coupe, Spider, and Racing.
As the first vehicle based on BYD’s e3 platform, all Denza Z models are equipped with three electric motors, one in the front and two at the rear, delivering a combined 1,582 hp (1,604 PS).
When equipped with the optional semi-slick tires, the Racing variant can accelerate from 0 to 62 mph in as little as 1.96 seconds with a top speed of 217 mph.
The Denza Z Coupe (shown above in Monte Carlo Orange) can hit 0 to 62 mph in 2.25 seconds, while the Spider variant takes about 2.3 seconds.
BYD’s e3 platform is the first in China to feature a fully in-house-developed steer-by-wire system, improving handling, control, and efficiency.
All Denza Z variants are powered by a 76 kWh BYD Blade Battery 2.0 battery. The Coupe has a WLTP range of 410 km (254 miles), while the Spider and Racing variants have WLTP ranges of 400 km (248 miles) and 380 km (236 miles), respectively.
The electric sports car also features BYD’s Disus-M magnetorheological intelligent suspension system, enabling it to absorb or jump over bumps, or even “dance.”
Based on an 800V architecture with BYD’s Flash Charging system, the Denza Z can recharge from 10% to 70% in as little as five minutes.
Measuring 4,780 mm in length, 1,990 mm in width, and 1,350 mm in height, with a wheelbase of 2,780 mm, the Denza Z is slightly bigger than the Porsche 911. It’s about the size of the Mercedes-AMG GT Coupe.
BYD’s electric supercar is considerably more powerful than both. In comparison, the Porsche 911 Turbo S delivers up to 701 hp, while the Mercedes-AMG GT Coupe offers up to 1,153 hp.
Inside, the Denza Z is equipped with BYD’s latest software and technology. The digital cockpit features an 8.8″ driver display and a 12.8″ infotainment screen with Google built in. It also includes a “theater-grade” sound system with 12 speakers in the Coupe and Racing variants, and 10 for the Spider.
In the UK, prices for the Denza Z Coupe start at £142,900 ($192,000), the Spider at £159,900 ($215,000), and the Racing version at £172,900 ($232,000).
will go on sale in late summer as the first Denza retail stores open. BYD plans to begin customer deliveries by the end of 2026.
While it’s significantly more than the Denza Z9 GT, the Z is still cheaper than the Porsche 911 Turbo S (£199,100).

title: Trek targets the fat tire e-bike boom with its new throttle-enabled Borrego+
content:
| Jul 9 2026 - 7:00 am PT
Trek is making a bigger push into the fast-growing fat tire e-bike segment with the launch of the new Borrego+, a Class 2 electric bike that blends adventure-ready capability with commuter-friendly practicality. The company is also offering a faster Borrego+ S variant for riders in markets where Class 3 e-bikes are permitted.
While Trek has long been known for
premium analog bikes and mid-drive electric bicycles,
the Borrego+ signals a move toward the increasingly popular throttle-equipped fat tire category that has largely been dominated by
direct-to-consumer brands
The new e-bike features a 750W rear hub motor producing 90 Nm of torque, paired with a 720 Wh removable battery rated for up to 60 miles (97 km) of range.
The standard Borrego+ is configured as a 20 mph Class 2 e-bike with both pedal assist and a thumb throttle, while the Borrego+ S raises the assisted top speed to 28 mph as a Class 3 model. Trek notes that the Class 3 version won’t be sold in California due to state regulations.
As expected from a fat tire model, the Borrego+ rolls on 4-inch-wide tires designed to improve traction on loose terrain while also including puncture protection for added durability.
An 80 mm suspension fork helps smooth rough roads and trails, and the upright riding position should appeal to riders looking for comfort over aggressive handling.
Trek has also equipped the Borrego+ with what looks to be a fairly practical feature set. A 440 lb (200 kg) carrying capacity, heavy-duty MIK-compatible rear rack, integrated accessory mounts, fenders, skid plate, brake light, and daytime-visible turn signals make it well-suited for everything from grocery runs to weekend adventures.
The bike also integrates with Trek’s Central app, adding features like a digital bike lock, battery alerts, custom ride modes, and range estimates. The removable top-eject battery is lockable and designed for easy charging on or off the bike.
The Borrego+ is available now through Trek dealers and the company’s website, giving one of the cycling industry’s biggest names a stronger foothold in one of the fastest-growing categories of the North American e-bike market.
Priced at $2,499, the Borrego+ now adds to Trek’s more affordable end of its lineup, focusing on budget shoppers who still want a name brand and nationwide servicing network.

title: Xiaomi says Sky Nomad is a series, not a new brand, teases SUV
content:
Fred Lambert
| Jul 9 2026 - 5:52 am PT
Xiaomi has answered the biggest open question about Sky Nomad: it’s a second product series under Xiaomi EV, not the standalone sub-brand that had been rumored for months.
The company also revealed the first clear look at the lineup’s debut model — a rugged, boxy extended-range SUV — in a set of official teaser posters.
confirmed the Sky Nomad name on Wednesday
, it pointedly did not say whether the effort was a new standalone brand or simply a new product series. Earlier reports had claimed the vehicles would drop the Xiaomi logo entirely.
On Thursday, Xiaomi EV settled it. “It is Sky Nomad (Xiaomi Pengcheng), a new series from Xiaomi EV,” the company wrote on its official Weibo account.
CEO Lei Jun framed the split by use case. The SU7/YU7 series is a “driver’s car,” he wrote, while Sky Nomad is an “intelligent, transformable large-space SUV” — “two different answers Xiaomi has delivered to different user needs over its more than five years of carmaking.”
That keeps the Xiaomi badge on the new SUV rather than spinning it off, and it lines Sky Nomad up as the company’s family-hauler answer to Li Auto and Huawei-backed Aito, which dominate China’s extended-range SUV market.
Xiaomi showed the SUV’s unobstructed side profile for the first time. Unlike the dynamic curves of the SU7 sedan and YU7 crossover, the new model has an especially rugged, boxy shape with an all-new front-end design.
Lei said the lineup was honed over three and a half years with the goal of creating a “living space,” where the interior isn’t fixed but changes with the owner’s needs. When parked, he said, the SUV can become “a studio for one, a café for two, a reception room for three, or a playground for the whole family.”
To pull that off, Xiaomi built an all-new platform from the ground up starting in early 2023 — the Xiaomi Kunlun architecture. A completely flat floor and long-rail seating design let the interior reconfigure on the fly, according to Lei, who argued that doing it well requires “AI technology + a smart ecosystem + intelligent manufacturing capabilities” at once.
It’s a familiar pitch in China, where “a refrigerator, a color TV, and a big sofa” have become standard family-SUV talking points. Lei’s twist is positioning the car as a mobile “Nth space” rather than just a way to get around.
The hardware story hasn’t changed from what leaked ahead of the reveal. The first model, internally codenamed Kunlun N3 and expected to launch as the N90, is a full-size SUV measuring more than 5.3 meters (17.4 feet) long.
It’s Xiaomi’s first vehicle that won’t be a pure EV. A battery pack of more than 70 kWh delivers 400 to 500 km (249-311 miles) of electric-only range, with a gasoline engine acting purely as a generator to stretch total range past 1,500 km. For context, that battery is larger than the 62.5 kWh pack in the base Tesla Model Y sold in China.
Pricing is expected to start as low as 200,000 yuan (~$29,400), undercutting the Li Auto L9 and Aito M9, which both sit above 250,000 yuan. If the rumors are true, it would be quite a feat. The move follows China’s Ministry of Industry and Information Technology
clearing Xiaomi to build extended-range vehicles
at its Beijing plant on June 10.
Sky Nomad isn’t a side project — it’s central to Xiaomi’s 2026 volume target. The company wants to deliver 550,000 vehicles this year, up about 34% from roughly 410,000 in 2025.
It has work to do. Xiaomi EV delivered 185,055 vehicles in the first half of 2026, only about 34% of the annual goal. Hitting the target means averaging roughly 60,000 deliveries a month in the second half — double the 30,000-plus monthly pace it’s been running, even with the
SU7 undercutting Tesla’s Model 3
YU7 undercutting the Model Y by $4,350
. A launch is still expected in the second half of 2026, but Lei again declined to give a date.
If you’re weighing how far EV range can stretch, remember the cheapest miles are the ones you generate at home with solar. With electricity rates climbing nearly 10% last year, home solar protects you against future rate increases. title: Meta 正式向开发者开放 Muse Spark AI 模型 API，加入 AI 商业化竞争
content:
7 月 9 日消息，Meta Platforms 今日正式向开发者开 Muse Spark AI 模型 API，同时发布了升级版 Muse Spark 1.1。此举意味着 Meta 正式加入 AI 模型商业化竞争，与 Anthropic、OpenAI 等公司一样，开始通过模型调用收费。
Meta 表示，Muse Spark 1.1 是公司迄今在真实世界编程（Coding）和 AI Agent（智能体）任务方面能力最强的模型，也是其打造“个人超级智能（Personal Superintelligence）”战略的重要组成部分。
据介绍，新版模型具备以下能力：
操作软件及调用外部工具；
理解文本、图片和视频内容；
在更少人工干预的情况下完成复杂的多步骤任务。
今年 4 月，Meta 首次发布了 Muse Spark。这是 Meta 去年组建“超级智能（Superintelligence）”团队后推出的首款文本推理模型，旨在缩小与竞争对手在 AI 领域的差距。
发布之初，Meta 仅向部分合作伙伴开放了应用程序接口（API）私测。API 是 AI 模型商业化的重要基础，它相当于连接开发者与 AI 模型的“数字桥梁”，开发者可将模型能力集成到自己的应用和软件中。
如今，美国开发者已可通过 Meta Model API 公开预览版使用 Muse Spark，对模型进行提示词测试、输出效果比较以及应用原型开发。
注册 API 的开发者可获得 20 美元免费额度，用于体验模型，之后则按调用量付费。Meta 公布的定价为：每百万输入 Token 收费 1.25 美元；每百万输出 Token 收费 4.25 美元。
这一价格高于 OpenAI 入门级 GPT-5 mini 以及 Anthropic 主打低成本的 Claude Haiku 4.5，但低于 Anthropic 高端模型 Claude Sonnet 4.6。
除了开放 API 外，Muse Spark 1.1 已在 Meta AI 应用和网页版中以 Thinking（思考）模式上线。
未来，该模型还将逐步取代目前驱动 WhatsApp、Instagram、Facebook 以及 Meta 智能眼镜聊天机器人的 Llama 系列模型。
IT之家注意到，此次发布紧随 Meta 本周二的另一项 AI 更新。当时，公司宣布将生成式 AI 功能扩展至旗下各款应用，并推出了来自 Meta Superintelligence Labs 的首款图像生成模型 Muse Image。

title: SpaceX 与 AI 公司排队造富，私人飞机行业意外先被新贵们带火了
content:
北京时间 7 月 9 日，据路透社报道，SpaceX 和其他 AI 公司的首次公开招股 (IPO) 创造的财富，推动了私人航空需求的新一轮增长。
航空律师阿曼达 · 阿普尔盖特 (Amanda Applegate) 上个月取消了年度假期，因为 AI 创业公司和 SpaceX 创造的大量财富，引发了一波科技投资者购买私人飞机的热潮，使她忙于处理大量飞机购买协议文件。这位驻克利夫兰的律师将这股热潮归因于科技行业少数几起重大“流动性事件”。
SpaceX 的 IPO 为该公司筹集了创纪录的 857 亿美元，并为员工和创始人创造了前所未有的财富。SpaceX 拥有 AI 公司 xAI。接下来有望进行大规模 IPO 的 AI 公司是 Anthropic 和 OpenAI。
SpaceX 及其他 AI 公司的风险投资人、董事会成员和早期员工，以及负责推动这些公司 IPO 的投行人士，正将新获得的财富投入私人航空领域，使其成为 AI 热潮最早受益的行业之一。
路透社上个月报道称，随着市场预计 AI 行业将催生新一批亿万富翁，越来越多豪华旅行公司开始将科技企业家作为重点客户。
“我认为，能够负担私人航空出行的人越来越多，而且这个群体似乎每天都在扩大。”阿普尔盖特说。今年迄今为止，她所在的公司 Soar Aviation Law 的业务量已跃升了 25%。该公司负责处理飞机购买及协议相关业务。
对许多人来说，私人航空的起步往往是从会员制或共享所有权项目开始的，之后再逐步过渡到拥有自己的飞机。航空情报公司 Jetnet 的数据显示，2026 年前五个月，全球通过共享所有权项目执行的航班量较 2025 年同期增长了 11.8%。
与此同时，由私人飞机机主运营的航班数量同比增长 13.4%，反映出随着人们对商业航班体验越来越不满，私人航空需求正全面上升。在全球最大的私人航空市场北美，这一增长既表明现有飞机所有者更频繁地升空飞行，也显示出新晋富裕买家正迈入拥有飞机的行列。
这股消费热潮反映出一种熟悉的规律。历史上，重大财富创造事件都会带动私人航空需求增长，例如股市大涨、IPO 或企业并购。JetNet 的数据显示，在互联网泡沫时期，公务机交付量增长了 24%。
这一次，私人航空热潮恰逢市场对 SpaceX 高度追捧之际。SpaceX 目前的市值约为 2 万亿美元。市场也普遍预计，OpenAI 和 Anthropic 未来也将完成大规模 IPO。
提供公务机共享所有权、租赁及会员服务的私人航空公司 Flexjet 已经注意到了这一变化。该公司的会员计划允许客户预付飞行小时费用，并按需使用私人飞机。
“白手起家的第一代财富人群，比如那些将从这些科技 IPO 中受益的人，正在让 Flexjet 的客户群体变得更加年轻。”总部位于克利夫兰的 Flexjet 销售执行副总裁
. Hanlon) 表示。
即使这些公司尚未完成上市，私募市场估值的飙升已经让许多投资者认为未来套现回报越来越确定，从而促使一些人在财富兑现事件发生之前，就提前进行大额消费。
一位来自加州的飞机经纪人表示：“过去 6 个月到 10 个月里，我遇到几位参与 SpaceX 投资的人，他们手里的钱多得不知道该怎么花。”由于涉及客户关系，这位经纪人要求匿名。
这位经纪人说，十年前，科技客户约占他业务的五分之一。如今，这一比例已占到约四分之三，并且他们正在迅速抢购稀缺的新型豪华飞机库存。他补充道：“我去年卖掉的飞机，如果放到今天，能多卖 10% 到 15%。”
JetNet 表示，超级富裕人口预计加速增长至 2028 年，这反映出 AI 带来的财富暴增正在产生直接影响。
据 Jetnet 旗下公司 WINGX 的数据，在 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 所在的旧金山，截至 6 月 14 日，公务机航班量在美国主要城市中增长最快，同比增幅约为 11%。WINGX 称，在 SpaceX 发射场附近的得克萨斯州布朗斯维尔，该公司 IPO 窗口期内的公务机航班量猛增 177%，达到 97 架次。

title: 全球首例：美国研究团队远程操控宇树 G1 人形机器人完成活体动物外科手术
content:
7 月 9 日消息，美国加利福尼亚大学圣迭戈分校（University of California San Diego）宣布，该校工程师团队和外科医生团队合作，利用两台宇树 G1 人形机器人，成功完成了两例
大型非灵长类哺乳动物的活体胆囊切除手术
两台机器人均由外科医生通过控制台进行远程操控实施手术。两例手术中，一例由单台机器人与外科医生协作完成，另一例则完全由外科医生远程操控两台机器人完成。相关研究成果已于当地时间 7 月 8 日发表在《Nature》。
研究团队指出，专用的手术系统通常配备三到四个机械臂、专用工具以及专有软件。这些系统重约 1800 磅（IT之家注：约 816.5 千克），需要庞大的团队进行安装调试，并且会占用手术室极大的空间，往往还需要对手术室进行专门改造才能将其容纳。相比之下，人形机器人不仅体积小巧，还可以移动，在偏远且医疗资源匮乏的地区尤为实用。
不过，远程操控人形机器人完成手术目前仍有许多问题需要解决。在手术过程中，机器人需要进行多次重新校准。因此，与使用现成的专用外科手术系统相比，本次手术操作耗费的时间要长得多。此外，远程操控带来的控制延迟等问题也需要进一步优化。
https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10796-x

title: 印度取消部分手机及电子设备零部件进口关税，利好苹果、小米等厂商
content:
7 月 9 日消息，据路透社报道，印度已取消部分手机及其他电子设备零部件的进口关税，免除了此前 7.5% 和 5% 的税率，此举有望帮助苹果、小米等企业进一步降低制造成本。
此次获得免税的产品包括：用于生产手机无线充电模块的关键零部件、医疗设备和汽车显示屏相关零部件，以及锂离子电芯等。该免税政策将持续至 2029 年 3 月 31 日。
商业咨询公司 Grant Thornton Bharat 合伙人马诺杰 · 米什拉（Manoj Mishra）表示，此举将有助于提升印度电子制造业的成本竞争力，提高本地增值率，并推动高价值智能手机及电子产品制造进一步实现本土化。
他还表示，对锂离子电芯制造实施免税政策，也有望吸引更多企业投资印度本土电池生产，带动消费电子和电动交通领域的发展。
印度政府计划到 2030 财年，将全国电子制造业规模扩大至 5000 亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 3.41 万亿元人民币）。
数据显示，过去十年间，印度智能手机产值增长了 28 倍，在 2024/25 财年达到 5.45 万亿卢比（现汇率约合 3902.96 亿元人民币）。

title: 安全公司曝光黑客山寨苹果 macOS 剪贴板管理软件 Maccy 官网，借此传播恶意木马
content:
7 月 9 日消息，安全公司 Jamf 发文，透露近期有黑客山寨苹果 macOS 平台知名剪贴板管理工具 Maccy 官网，诱骗用户下载安装带有恶意木马 PamStealer 安装脚本的软件包。
Jamf 表示，PamStealer 的攻击分为两个阶段。黑客首先搭建仿冒 Maccy 官网，并利用搜索引擎竞价排名机制刊载广告提升山寨网站权重，以便吸引毫不知情的受害者点击网站下载恶意软件包。
在用户下载启动恶意软件包后，其中内置的 AppleScript 脚本会进行环境检测，确保脚本并非运行于沙盒环境中，之后便会调用 macOS 的 PAM 认证机制，唤出系统原生管理员密码输入窗口，要求用户输入管理员密码。
在用户输入管理员密码后，软件包便会从黑客部署的服务器上下载 PamStealer 恶意木马，相应木马使用 Rust 编写，能够伪装成苹果 macOS 文件管理器“访达（Finder）”，在运行后便会搜集用户设备上浏览器数据、加密货币钱包、剪贴板等敏感信息，并在完成加密后上传至黑客的服务器，以便黑客后续向受害者发起勒索。

title: 消息称特斯拉三代擎天柱人形机器人初步定型，马斯克放话达不成产能目标就开掉整个采购团队
content:
7 月 9 日消息，据晚点 LatePost 今日报道，多位特斯拉供应链人士透露，特斯拉近期终于下发具体的 Optimus 零件采购指引，要求供应商在今年 9 月将产能提到 1000 台 / 周，
随后在年底提升到 2000-2500 台 / 周
两位接近供应商的人士说，特斯拉会提前 2 个月左右下订单，现在他们已经看到 8 月数百台的具体订单。
以此估算，特斯拉的供应商们将在年底具备每年向特斯拉供应
10 万台 Optimus 零部件
报道还称，给供应链下单前，在六月底的一次高管会上，马斯克评审通过了 Optimus 的最新版本，这意味着 Optimus Gen 3 在研发三年多后，终于将走出实验室，
两位供应链人士都说，在这次会议上，马斯克要求团队在年底前实现产能目标，
否则他会开掉整个 Optimus 采购团队
—— 这比任何订单和预期指引都更能给供应链、资本市场甚至整个机器人产业信心。
IT之家注意到，马斯克 7 月 1 日发布了一张大合照，并配文称，在弗里蒙特工厂参观 Optimus 擎天柱机器人生产线。
有网友猜测，特斯拉近期减少 Optimus 公开演示，可能是因为量产进度已超出市场预期。对此，马斯克回应称：“不，
Optimus 的生产一开始会极其缓慢
，因为一切都是全新的。这不像制造汽车。”
特斯拉弗里蒙特工厂曾用于生产 Model S 和 Model X 两款车型，如今已改造为人形机器人生产线。今年 5 月，
最后一辆 Model S 和最后一辆 Model X 已在弗里蒙特工厂下线
马斯克在今年 3 月谈到 Optimus 3 机器人时
，将其描述为“迄今为止全球最先进的机器人”，并表示：“我还没见过任何能媲美 Optimus 3 的机器人演示。”在产能规划上，马斯克表示，低产量生产可在夏季启动，高产量生产则会在 2027 年展开。
而在今年 4 月的 2026 年第一季度财报电话会上
，马斯克确认 Optimus 机器人将于 7 月下旬或 8 月在弗里蒙特投产。不过，马斯克同时泼了冷水，直言 Optimus 初期产量会“相当缓慢”。原因在于，Optimus 对应的是一条全新生产线，还涉及 10000 个独特零部件，今年到底能爬到什么产量，“根本不可能预测”。

title: 索尼推出黑卡 RX10V 长焦相机：2010 万像素 1 英寸传感器、等效 24-600mm 镜头，国行 15199 元
content:
7 月 9 日消息，索尼现已发布黑卡 RX10V 长焦相机，将于 8 月初（下月）上市，国行 15199 元。
该机搭载等效约 24-600mm 蔡司 Vario-Sonnar T * 镜头（F2.4-4.0 光圈），广角端最近 3 cm 微距对焦，长焦端最高 0.49 倍放大倍率。
该机握柄与拨轮迎来重新设计，同步拿掉了机顶的显示屏，机身提供独立 AF-ON 按钮、双控制拨轮，配备 368 万点（120fps 刷新率）EVF 电子取景器，屏幕从 144 万点 LCD 升级为 162 万点 LCD。数据传输接口从 Micro USB 升级为 USB-C 10 Gbps。
该机搭载 2010 万像素 1 英寸堆栈式 Exmor RS CMOS 传感器，匹配双 BIONZ XR 图像处理器，ISO 范围覆盖 100 至 12800（可扩展至 ISO 64），连拍从每秒 24 张升级至每秒 30 张，每秒最高 60 次 AF / AE（自动对焦 / 自动曝光）计算。
同时，该机升级支持 AI 智能主体识别对焦（支持人类、动物、鸟类、昆虫、汽车、火车、飞机和自动），提供实时识别对焦 / 实时追踪（约 575 个相位检测自动对焦点）。并提供了创意外观（Creative Look）配置文件。
视频方面，该机支持无裁切 4K 60p 及裁切 4K120p，支持 4:2:2 10 bit 色深、S-Cinetone 、S-Log3，支持监看 LUT 及 LUT 导入、支持视频增强防抖模式（Active Mode）和自动取景构图、支持 4K30p 直播推流。
其他方面，该机电池升级为 NP-FZ100（CIPA 标准约 630 张）、支持 5GHz 双频 Wi-Fi，内置 MI 数字音频接口。

title: 调查：亚马逊搜索结果中被推荐的 U 盘，约 20% 被消费者质疑容量造假
content:
7 月 9 日消息，日经新闻的一项调查显示，在亚马逊搜索结果中被推荐的 USB 闪存盘（U 盘）中，约 20% 存在多条评论指出其实际容量与标注不符的情况。这暴露出平台对部分卖家审核不足的问题，也让消费者面临数据安全风险。
除了可能导致数据丢失外，这类假冒 U 盘还可能预装恶意软件。即使消费者最终退货，也可能在使用过程中遭受病毒感染，带来持续的安全隐患。
调查发现，中国品牌 U 盘很少在线下大型商场销售，而亚马逊已成为其最主要的销售渠道。例如，日本乐天市场（Rakuten Ichiba）上的一些大型卖家，就是从亚马逊购买这些产品后再进行转售。
IT之家注意到，日经新闻于今年 6 月底，对亚马逊日本站和美国站搜索“USB memory”时推荐的各 100 款产品（共计 200 款）进行了分析，结果发现约 20% 的产品存在多条评论质疑其存储容量存在造假。
其中，美国站情况更为严重，接近 40% 的推荐产品收到相关投诉；日本站这一比例则略低于 10%。
由于亚马逊搜索结果会受到时间和用户购买记录等因素影响，日经新闻还将此次结果与今年 4 月的一次调查进行了对比，发现整体趋势几乎没有变化。为尽量减少干扰，调查未统计赞助广告商品。
为了验证评论内容，日经新闻还从亚马逊购买了 5 款价格约为市场平均水平一半的中国品牌 U 盘，并委托日本数据恢复公司 AI Data 进行检测。检测结果显示，5 款产品的实际容量均低于宣传标称值。
调查发现，涉及容量造假的 220 条差评并不仅集中在一些不知名中国品牌上，也出现在部分知名品牌产品页面。例如，日本铠侠（Kioxia）和亚马逊自有品牌 Amazon Basics 的商品评论中，也有消费者表示，自己购买的是正品，却收到假冒产品。
铠侠方面表示：“我们已经注意到市场上存在假冒产品，并提醒消费者提高警惕。”
报道指出，这一问题部分源于亚马逊 Marketplace（第三方卖家平台）的运营机制。该平台提供在同一页面上从某一商品的多个卖家之间进行选择的选项。报价最低的卖家往往会被置于更显眼的位置，这就给假冒商品供应商提供了以假充真发货的机会。
对于普通消费者而言，除了查看页面侧边栏字体较小的卖家信息外，几乎没有其他方式判断商品来源。
日本知识产权保护组织 Union des Fabricants Tokyo 秘书长 Takayuki Tsutsumi 表示：“不断有卖家滥用这一机制，借助正品积累的信誉销售假冒商品，这种情况层出不穷。”
报道称，亚马逊会通过交易信息和视频通话等方式审核卖家身份，但面对平台数量庞大的第三方商家，很难做到全面且精准的审核。
长期关注消费者权益的日本律师 Takaaki Someya 表示：“即使是需要品牌授权才能销售的商品，也曾出现假货通过正品商品页面在亚马逊销售的情况。监管部门应制定更严格的规定，要求平台加强卖家审核。”
对此，亚马逊日本发言人回应称：“商品安全和真实性始终是亚马逊最重视的问题。我们正在调查相关情况，如有必要，将采取适当措施。”

title: 马斯克：SpaceX 上市已让公司数千名员工成为百万富翁
content:
7 月 9 日消息，埃隆 · 马斯克（Elon Musk）表示，SpaceX 创纪录的估值很可能已经让公司数千名员工跻身百万富翁行列。
在当地时间周三播出的《The Sean Hannity Show》节目中，马斯克被问及一位前 SpaceX 焊工的故事。据报道，这名员工曾获得公司股票，在 SpaceX 于今年 6 月上市、市值约达 2 万亿美元后，其持有股份价值已超过 100 万美元。
对此，马斯克在电话连线中对代班主持人、得州州长格雷格 · 阿博特（Greg Abbott）表示：“不仅仅是一位焊工，而是几千名生产线员工。如果他们加入公司的时间比较早，那么他们持有的股票现在很可能都已经价值超过 100 万美元。”
作为特斯拉和 SpaceX CEO，马斯克本人也因 SpaceX 上市，个人财富一度突破 1 万亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 6.81 万亿元人民币）。
他表示：“我一直秉持一个理念，那就是公司里的每个人都应该获得公司股票，这样他们就能分享公司成长带来的收益。”
“这也能很好地实现利益一致。公司发展得越好，员工也会随之受益。”他补充道。
在 SpaceX 于 6 月 12 日上市前，Pre-IPO 股票交易平台 Hill Markets 创始人安德鲁 · 本森（Andrew Benson）曾预测，此次 IPO 将创造约 4400 名百万富翁，以及 400 多名身家过亿美元的富豪。
Business Insider 此前采访的一名前 SpaceX 员工透露，公司会在员工入职时授予股票期权，之后每年的绩效考核以及晋升时，也会继续发放股票奖励。
这位前员工还表示，公司通常每年举行两次私募流动性交易（Liquidity Event），允许员工将部分持股出售给公司或外部投资者，以实现套现。
除了员工持股外，SpaceX 总裁兼 COO 格温妮 · 肖特韦尔（Gwynne Shotwell）及其丈夫也希望让更多人分享 SpaceX 的发展成果。两人已向“特朗普账户（Trump Accounts）”项目捐赠了约 3 亿美元的 SpaceX 股票。该项目由美国政府推出，计划为 2025 年初至 2028 年底出生的每一位美国儿童开设账户，并存入 1000 美元启动资金。
SpaceX 股票上市首日发行价为 135 美元，上市后数日曾涨至 200 多美元，不过截至周三收盘，股价已回落至 148 美元以下。
除了谈及 IPO 带来的财富效应，马斯克还在节目中介绍了 SpaceX 的长期目标。他说，希望 10 年后，SpaceX 能够“在月球建立基地”，并让“数千人，甚至数万人”前往月球。
马斯克表示：“我们希望把科幻作品里的东西变成现实，而不是停留在幻想中。”
他还介绍称，SpaceX 的“星舰（Starship）”系统最终将具备向月球运输数万吨物资的能力，从而在月球建立一座真正意义上的城市，并最终在火星建造城市。
马斯克最后表示，如果一切顺利，SpaceX 有望在约 5 年后首次将人类送上火星，并在 10 至 12 年内将数千人送往这颗红色星球。

title: FZI und DFKI entwickeln neue Ansätze für resiliente Kommunikation in Krisenlagen
content:
FZI und DFKI entwickeln neue Ansätze für resiliente Kommunikation in Krisenlagen

Vertreter der Cyberagentur sowie der Forschungspartner FZI und DFKI nach der Unterzeichnung der Projekturkunden zum offiziellen Start der Forschungsphase des Programms MoIn-LaMAS am 9. Juli 2026.
Cyberagentur
| Copyright:
Cyberagentur
Cyberagentur beauftragt Forschung zu autonomen robotischen Kommunikationsnetzen
FZI und DFKI entwickeln neue Ansätze für resiliente Kommunikation in Krisenlagen
Die Agentur für Innovation in der Cybersicherheit GmbH (Cyberagentur) hat am 9. Juli 2026 gemeinsam mit dem FZI Forschungszentrum Informatik und dem Deutschen Forschungszentrum für Künstliche Intelligenz (DFKI) den offiziellen Start der Forschungsphase des Programms MoIn-LaMAS besiegelt. Ziel ist die Entwicklung autonomer robotischer Systeme, die in Krisenlagen selbstständig eine belastbare Kommunikationsinfrastruktur aufbauen und aufrechterhalten können.
Start für eine neue Generation autonomer Kommunikationsinfrastrukturen
Mit der Unterzeichnung der Projekturkunden hat die Cyberagentur am 9. Juli 2026 den offiziellen Start der Forschungsphase ihres Forschungsprogramms MoIn-LaMAS – Mobile Infrastruktur: Lageorientierung für Mobile Autonome Systeme eingeleitet. Gemeinsam mit zwei leistungsfähigen Forschungspartnern sollen in den kommenden zwei Jahren Technologien entstehen, die Kommunikationsnetze auch dort aufbauen und stabil halten können, wo bestehende Infrastrukturen ausgefallen oder nicht vorhanden sind.
Die Urkunden unterzeichneten auf Seiten der Cyberagentur die kaufmännische Geschäftsführerin Bettina Bubnys sowie die Referatsleiterin Unbemannte Systeme und Robotik, Dr. Eva-Maria Heinke. Für die Forschungspartner unterschrieben Johannes Mangler, Technischer Projektleiter des FZI-Vorhabens R.O.A.M. – Roboterteams für Autonome Lagebilder und Mobile Ad-hoc-Netzwerke, sowie Dr.-Ing. Malte Wirkus, Projektleiter des DFKI-Projekts ART-LaKo – Autonomes Roboter-Team zur Erstellung und Aufrechterhaltung eines Lagebildes und einer Kommunikationsinfrastruktur in Krisenfällen.
Ziel ist ein Demonstrator, der die Praxistauglichkeit der entwickelten Technologien unter realitätsnahen Einsatzbedingungen nachweist.
Kommunikation wird Teil des autonomen Lagebildes
MoIn-LaMAS verfolgt einen Forschungsansatz, der sich deutlich von bestehenden Arbeiten im Bereich autonomer Robotik und mobiler Kommunikationsnetze unterscheidet. Während viele internationale Forschungsprojekte Navigation, Verbundsteuerung oder Funkkommunikation getrennt betrachten, verbindet MoIn-LaMAS diese Disziplinen zu einem integrierten Gesamtsystem.
Im Mittelpunkt steht die Entwicklung autonomer Systeme, die nicht nur ihre Umgebung erfassen, sondern gleichzeitig die Qualität der Kommunikationsverbindungen bewerten und ihre Positionen entsprechend anpassen. Faktoren wie Funkreichweite, Bandbreite, Störungen oder Geländeprofile werden Bestandteil des digitalen Lagebildes. Auf dieser Grundlage entscheiden die Systeme eigenständig, wie Kommunikationsknoten optimal verteilt oder verlagert werden müssen.
Damit wird Kommunikation selbst zu einer entscheidenden Informationsquelle für die autonome Entscheidungsfindung.
Heterogene Roboterverbünde schaffen resiliente Netze
Ein weiterer wissenschaftlicher Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Zusammenarbeit unterschiedlicher autonomer Plattformen. Luftgestützte und bodengebundene Systeme bilden gemeinsam einen heterogenen Verbund, der Kommunikationsnetze flexibel aufbauen, erweitern und rekonfigurieren kann.
Dadurch entsteht eine mobile Infrastruktur, die sich kontinuierlich an veränderte Einsatzbedingungen anpasst. Fällt ein Kommunikationsknoten aus oder verändert sich die Lage, organisiert sich das Netzwerk selbstständig neu. Dieser Ansatz erhöht die Robustheit gegenüber Ausfällen und ermöglicht Kommunikationsverbindungen auch in schwer zugänglichen oder stark gestörten Einsatzräumen.
Die erwarteten Forschungsergebnisse besitzen insbesondere für den Bevölkerungsschutz, den Katastrophenschutz sowie den Grenzschutz ein erhebliches Innovationspotenzial.
„Mit MoIn-LaMAS erforschen wir Technologien, die Kommunikationsinfrastrukturen nicht nur ersetzen, sondern sie unter dynamischen Bedingungen eigenständig aufbauen und erhalten können. Die Verbindung von Robotik, Künstlicher Intelligenz und Kommunikation eröffnet völlig neue Möglichkeiten für zukünftige Einsatzszenarien“, sagt Thilo Behrendt, Leiter des Programms in der Cyberagentur.
Zwei Forschungsansätze für ein gemeinsames Ziel
Die beiden ausgewählten Forschungsvorhaben verfolgen unterschiedliche wissenschaftliche Ansätze und ergänzen sich gegenseitig.
Das FZI Forschungszentrum Informatik entwickelt mit R.O.A.M. Verfahren für autonome Roboterteams, die Lagebilder erstellen und mobile Ad-hoc-Kommunikationsnetze aufbauen.
Das Deutsche Forschungszentrum für Künstliche Intelligenz (DFKI) erforscht mit ART-LaKo autonome Robotersysteme, die gleichzeitig Lagebilder erzeugen sowie Kommunikationsinfrastrukturen in dynamischen Krisenszenarien aufrechterhalten.
Beide Projekte leisten damit einen wesentlichen Beitrag zur Entwicklung zukünftiger Schlüsseltechnologien für eine resiliente und adaptive Kommunikationsversorgung unter anspruchsvollen Einsatzbedingungen.
Weitere Informationen:
https://www.cyberagentur.de/programme/moin-lamas/
Pressekontakt:
Hintergrund: Cyberagentur
Die Agentur für Innovation in der Cybersicherheit GmbH (Cyberagentur) wurde im Jahr 2020 als vollständige Inhouse-Gesellschaft des Bundes unter der gemeinsamen Federführung des Bundesministeriums der Verteidigung und des Bundesministeriums des Inneren durch die Bundesregierung mit dem Ziel gegründet, einen im Bereich der Cybersicherheit anwendungsstrategiebezogenen und ressortübergreifenden Blick auf die Innere und Äußere Sicherheit einzunehmen. Vor diesem Hintergrund bezweckt die Arbeit der Cyberagentur maßgeblich eine institutionalisierte Durchführung von hochinnovativen Vorhaben, die mit einem hohen Risiko bezüglich der Zielerreichung behaftet sind, gleichzeitig aber ein sehr hohes Disruptionspotenzial bei Erfolg innehaben können.
Die Cyberagentur ist Bestandteil der Nationalen Sicherheitsstrategie der Bundesrepublik Deutschland.
Der Cyberagentur stehen als Geschäftsführung Prof. Dr. Christian Hummert als Forschungsdirektor und Bettina Bubnys als kaufmännische Geschäftsführung vor.

title: TUM Venture Labs und Durst kooperieren bei Robotik und KI
content:
TUM Venture Labs und Durst kooperieren bei Robotik und KI
Die TUM Venture Labs und die Durst Group starten eine mehrjährige Zusammenarbeit im Bereich Robotik, KI und Automatisierung für die industrielle Produktion. Damit wird Durst Teil des Innovationsökosystems rund um die Technische Universität München (TUM) und zugleich der erste italienische Partner im Netzwerk der TUM Venture Labs. Mit den Gründungsteams im Venture Lab Robotics / AI werden die Partner an Lösungen für Fragen aus der Industriepraxis arbeiten.
Durst baut intelligente Systeme für den industriellen Digitaldruck und die Produktion. Das Familienunternehmen mit Hauptsitz in Brixen ist mit dem Einsatz von Digitalisierung, Automatisierung und Systemintegration ein Vorreiter bei der Transformation hin zu vernetzten, datengetriebenen Produktionsumgebungen.
Die zwölf TUM Venture Labs sind auf je ein bedeutendes Technologiefeld spezialisiert. Den Gründungsteams bieten sie auf diesem Gebiet eine unmittelbare Anbindung an die Spitzenforschung, spezifische technische Infrastruktur, maßgeschneiderte Ausbildungsprogramme, Expertise für den jeweiligen Markt und eine globale Vernetzung mit der Branche und Kapitalgeberinnen und Kapitalgebern. Das Venture Lab Robotics / AI bündelt Kompetenzen in Robotik, Künstlicher Intelligenz, Embedded Systems und industrieller Automatisierung.
Im Zentrum der Zusammenarbeit stehen ein regelmäßiger fachlicher Austausch, Industry-Challenges, bei denen Gründungsteams Lösungen erarbeiten, sowie die gemeinsame Gestaltung von Entrepreneurship- und Innovationsformaten. Durst bringt reale Fragestellungen aus der Produktion ein – dort, wo Automatisierung, Robotik, KI-basierte Prozesssteuerung und physische Abläufe zusammenkommen. Die Lösungen sollen sich nicht nur auf die Druckindustrie, sondern auch auf angrenzende und branchenübergreifende Anwendungen beziehen.
„Isolierte Maschinen waren gestern. Produktion wird vernetzt, lernend, autonom. Wir bauen die Systems dafür“, sagt Christoph Gamper, CEO und Miteigentümer der Durst Group. „München ist für uns kein Zufall. Hier sitzen Talente, die aus der Forschung heraus in kurzen Zyklen Produkte entwickeln. Genau diese Energie suchen wir.“ Für Durst entstehe dadurch eine neue Schnittstelle zu jungen Unternehmen, Forschenden, Studierenden und Industriepartnern. „Robotik und KI verändern die Produktion. Das ist entschieden. Offen ist nur das Tempo. Dafür braucht es neue Formen der Zusammenarbeit: Industrie, Forschung, Start-ups und Talente an einem Tisch.“
„Robotics und AI entfalten ihr Potenzial dort, wo sie auf reale industrielle Herausforderungen treffen“, sagt Dr. Philipp Gerbert, CEO der TUM Venture Labs. „Die Partnerschaft mit Durst verbindet technologische Exzellenz, Unternehmertum und konkrete Anwendung. So entsteht ein starkes Umfeld für neue Automatisierungslösungen rund um die digitale Produktion.“

title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
content:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power.
Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can sustain a chain reaction, by the nation’s 250th birthday. And just in time, four reactors did so.
It was a lofty goal, and seeing not just three but four companies meet it is certainly a positive sign for emerging nuclear technologies at a time when the world is facing increased need to increase electricity supply and address climate change with emissions-free technologies.
But achieving criticality doesn’t mean a reactor is ready to provide electricity for the grid (or at all, for that matter). Let’s untangle what this program’s success could mean for nuclear power in the US, and where these companies might go from here.
The Reactor Pilot Program essentially opened a special door for prototype reactors to fast-track development. In August, the US Department of Energy selected
11 reactor projects
for the program and offered them land and support from the national labs system. These are all microreactors; the large light-water reactors that dominate the grid today are tens or even hundreds of times their size.
Antares Nuclear was the
first to achieve criticality
, reaching the milestone in June in its Mark-0 test reactor. Reactors from Valar Atomics, Deployable Energy, and Aalo Atomics followed. (Aalo hit the mark in the
early hours of July 4
—an inspiring example of just barely meeting a deadline.)
The speed with which these companies hit this milestone is impressive, especially in an industry known for massive projects that frequently blow past deadlines and stated budgets. (Valar, Antares, and Aalo were all founded in 2023, and Deployable started in 2025.) But reaching criticality and running a reactor that can produce electricity are two totally different things.
All these reactors reached what’s called zero-power criticality. Basically, it’s a test of whether you can start a nuclear chain reaction, with no meaningful power coming from the reactor. “A zero-power-criticality test can be achieved without making real engineering progress on fuel or design,” Kathryn Huff, a former assistant secretary for nuclear energy and chair of the Department of Nuclear Engineering and Engineering Physics of the University of Wisconsin–Madison, said on
podcast earlier this year.
Now, with the completion of this program, the companies will need to continue their work to make power, which could involve some big technical challenges. In some cases they’ll need to add significant equipment, like the cooling systems to transfer the heat out of the reactor core.
The companies are projecting aggressive timelines moving forward. Aalo says it’s already begun work on the second reactor and plans to produce 10 megawatts of electricity to power an on-site data center
. Deployable Energy says it plans to deploy commercial reactors
I tend to take timelines from startups, especially in nuclear, with a grain of salt. Not only are these remarkably complex technical machines, but companies often run into problems outside their own control, like regulatory challenges—which these new projects could soon face.
The Nuclear Regulatory Commission is in charge of civilian and commercial nuclear use in the US, and historically, the process to get nuclear reactors approved has been quite slow.
The agency did propose a new framework for microreactor approvals earlier this year, which is designed to speed up the process—but it’s yet to be seen how quickly things will move. (And it’s worth noting here that some nuclear experts have questioned whether the agency under the Trump administration is loosening nuclear rules too much.)
Some nuclear supporters aren’t applauding the microreactor milestone. Federal focus on the program is an “unhelpful diversion” from goals to meaningfully increase nuclear capacity, according to one
Criticality is a big first step, but a lot will still have to happen for any of these microreactors to come online, much less for these small reactors to be a significant source of electricity for the grid.
This article is from The Spark,
MIT Technology Review
’s weekly climate newsletter. To receive it in your inbox every Wednesday,

Researchers are starting to explore the tools and systems we need to develop to cool down the planet.
Startup Rock Zero is commercializing the research, which could cut costs and carbon emissions from lithium production.

title: The Download: a nuclear landmark, and China eyes Nvidia chips
content:
This is today's edition of
The Download
our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what's going on in the world of technology.
—Casey Crownhart
I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power.
Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can sustain a chain reaction, by the nation’s 250th birthday. And just in time, not just three, but four reactors did so.
It’s a positive sign for nuclear technologies at a time of increasing need for electricity and emissions-free energy sources. But achieving criticality doesn’t mean a reactor is ready to provide electricity for the grid (or at all, for that matter).
Here’s what the milestone could mean for nuclear power in the US—and where the four companies might go next
is from The Spark, our weekly climate tech newsletter.
to receive it in your inbox every Wednesday.
The must-reads
I’ve combed the internet to find you today’s most fun/important/scary/fascinating stories about technology.
1 China plans to let its top AI firms buy Nvidia H200 chips
Alibaba, ByteDance, and DeepSeek are set to get permission. (
+ China had previously withheld approval despite US authorization.
2 NATO is building a network to stop Russian attackers in their tracks
It will use sensors, drones, satellites, and AI to detect them. (
Business Insider
+ Troops are donning odd camouflage to elude drones.
+ The US wants cheaper drones as Iran's wrecking its Reapers.
Ars Technica
3 Researchers have a new idea to fight future El Niños: dimming the sun
Deflecting solar energy could cool the ocean and mitigate the risks. (
+ But there could be unexpected consequences.
New Scientist
+ And geoengineering as a field is getting a reality check.
MIT Technology Review
4 Meta is patenting an AI device that records users to analyse emotions
It ostensibly aims to tailor workout plans to the user’s mood. (
+ AI memory is privacy’s next frontier.
MIT Technology Review
5 Chipmakers are going vertical as Moore’s Law slows
They’re stacking transistors to keep chips advancing. (
+ IBM is betting on the technique.
MIT Technology Review
6 Ivy League students suspected of AI cheating saw scores fall in person
Ars Technica
+ AI giants want to take over the classroom.
MIT Technology Review
7 A new study says parents’ phone addictions damage bonds with kids
It can exacerbate “insecure attachment” for life. (
+ And make children more anxious and avoidant.
8 A judge approved Musk’s $1.5 million Twitter settlement with the SEC
Despite what she called “serious misgivings” and “red flags.” (
+ Musk was accused of skirting stock disclosure rules.
9 Shoebox-sized “detector satellites” could find nuclear bombs in space
Cubesats carrying the detector could sense a bomb’s radiation. (
+ Russia is suspected of developing space-based nukes.
10 A World Cup match drove Google Search traffic to a new record
The milestone came after Argentina’s comeback against Egypt. (
Quote of the day
—President Donald Trump tells the public where to find his insights on the dangers of communism,
One More Thing
Paleontologists aren’t easily deterred by evolutionary dead ends or a sparse fossil record. And in the last few years, they’ve developed a new trick for turning back time and studying prehistoric animals: building experimental robotic models of them.
In the absence of a living specimen, an ambling, flying, swimming, or slithering automaton is the next best thing for studying the behavior of extinct organisms. Learning more about how they moved can in turn shed light on their lives, such as their historic ranges and feeding habits. Scientists can simply sit back and observe their behavior in different environments.
Read the full story on the rise of paleo-inspired robots—and four examples that are shedding light on creatures of yore
We can still have nice things
A place for comfort, fun, and distraction to brighten up your day. (Got any ideas?
Drop me a line
Georgia Hill’s monochrome artworks
are filled with visual harmony.
+ AI has salvaged text from a
papyrus scroll burned to a crisp
when Mount Vesuvius erupted 2,000 years ago.
+ Rare images taken by a Japanese space probe show a near-Earth asteroid
resembling a cuddly snowman
+ “Another One Bites the Bee Gees” smoothly merges two classic tracks with a 4/4 time signature into
the perfect song for applying CPR
Plus: Meta is pausing an AI training program that tracks workers’ keystrokes.
Plus: Anthropic has called for a global slowdown in AI development.
Plus: NASA unveiled plans for three uncrewed missions to the Moon this year.
Plus: SpaceX is now valued higher than Amazon.
